vfCrossroadsRelease.title: Virtua Fighter Crossroads Release Date and Platforms
vfCrossroadsRelease.intro: The core launch facts for SEGA and RGG Studio's new Virtua Fighter entry, separating the confirmed 2027 window from launch details that have not been published yet.

facts.label: Release Window
facts.details: SEGA lists Virtua Fighter Crossroads for a 2027 launch window.

facts.label: Official Title
facts.details: Unveiled as the official title of the New Virtua Fighter Project and the next mainline entry in the series.

facts.label: Developer
facts.details: RGG Studio is building the city of Vilasapara, the story-driven single-player structure, and the evolved combat experience.

facts.label: Publisher
facts.details: SEGA runs the official website, news pages, trailer releases, and social channels for the game.

facts.label: Platform Direction
facts.details: Producer and creative director Riichiro Yamada describes the project as primarily aimed at home play rather than arcades.

facts.label: Named Platforms
facts.details: The exact platform list has not been published on the official website or announcement pages.

facts.label: Launch Details
facts.value: Exact date, price, editions, preorder, and system requirements: TBA
facts.details: These details will be filled after SEGA publishes store pages or a dedicated release-date announcement.

vfCrossroadsTrailers.title: Virtua Fighter Crossroads Trailers and Showcase
vfCrossroadsTrailers.intro: A clean path through the official Virtua Fighter Crossroads reveal cycle, from the 2027 title reveal to Vilasapara, Cielo, the four-protagonist structure, and the newly revealed Bakunawa Killer.

videos.title: VIRTUA FIGHTER CROSSROADS | Official Reveal Trailer
videos.focus: Official title reveal and 2027 launch window
confirms: The New Virtua Fighter Project is now titled VIRTUA FIGHTER CROSSROADS.
The new direction blends cinematic story scenes with Virtua Fighter combat.

videos.title: VIRTUA FIGHTER CROSSROADS SHOWCASE
videos.focus: Developer overview of Vilasapara and combat evolution
confirms: Producer and creative director Riichiro Yamada and the development team explain the game's direction.
The showcase covers Vilasapara, the city where the story takes place.
The showcase introduces the evolved combat system and the fighting-adventure structure.

videos.title: VIRTUA FIGHTER CROSSROADS | Cielo Story Trailer
videos.focus: Cielo, Vilasapara, and the story-mode setup
confirms: Cielo is one of four new protagonists.
The story centers on interconnected lives, branching paths, and dramatic fights.
Vilasapara's fighting culture, crime pressure, and Vila Fight Fest conflict are central to the setup.

videos.title: VIRTUA FIGHTER CROSSROADS | Bakunawa Killer New Trailer
confirms: Bakunawa Killer is a new character from Cielo's story.
Bakunawa Killer is playable in Battle Mode.
The trailer connects Pai Chan and Cielo to the pursuit of the masked attacker.

videos.title: VIRTUA FIGHTER CROSSROADS | LEGACY Official AMV
confirms: SEGA is positioning Crossroads as a bold new chapter for Virtua Fighter.
The video supports the game's identity as a modern continuation of the long-running 3D fighting series.

vfCrossroadsStory.title: Virtua Fighter Crossroads Story Mode and Vilasapara
vfCrossroadsStory.intro: Virtua Fighter Crossroads is a fighting adventure, not just a traditional fighting campaign. The story takes place in Vilasapara, where player actions, victories, and defeats shape how the four-protagonist narrative unfolds.

chapters.title: A City Built Around Fighting
chapters.summary: The story is set in 20XX in Vilasapara, a fictional Southeast Asian metropolis where the Arma Carta ended an era of bloodshed and firearms are punished by death.
details: Crime syndicates operate under a fragile balance.
President Bato promotes Vila Fight Fest as a national sport and an economic solution.
A series of attacks on martial artists is tied to the figure known as the Bakunawa Killer.

chapters.title: Vilasapara
chapters.summary: Vilasapara is known as a fighting town and includes several distinct areas, from a walled city center to entertainment and resort districts.
details: The city is designed by RGG Studio as a living setting for exploration.
Players can uncover side stories and quests tied to residents and locations.
Developer interviews describe Vilasapara as a large city with multiple substantial districts.

chapters.title: Four Intersecting Stories
chapters.summary: The main scenario follows four new protagonists with different lives, goals, and reasons for coming to Vilasapara.
details: Cielo is the first protagonist players control in single-player.
The four protagonists' stories overlap, cross, and diverge across the city.
Each protagonist is designed with a different story tone and background.

chapters.title: Branching Paths
chapters.summary: The official site frames actions, victories, and defeats as major story-shaping forces.
details: The game is designed around more than simply watching story scenes between fights.
Developer interviews describe a structure built for different experiences across playthroughs.
Story Mode and versus play share the same core control foundation.

chapters.title: Side Stories and City Activities
chapters.summary: Exploration across Vilasapara leads to side stories, quests, and grounded activities connected to the city and its people.
details: The game aims to deliver a substantial single-player experience.
Side content is built around the world of Vilasapara rather than random filler activities.
Legacy Virtua Fighter characters can appear through story and side content.

chapters.title: Story Battles and Battle Mode
chapters.summary: Story Mode includes dramatic showdowns, multi-opponent brawls, and boss battles, while Battle Mode supports direct one-on-one fights.
details: Story battles are woven into the narrative instead of existing as detached arcade matches.
Battle Mode supports one-on-one matches both online and offline.
The combat keeps Virtua Fighter's punch, kick, and guard philosophy while rebuilding parts of the system.

chapters.title: Narrative Team
chapters.summary: The story is being developed with international writers and RGG Studio scenario staff.
details: David Hayter is credited for worldbuilding supervision.
Brad Kane is credited as lead writer.
Tsuyoshi Furuta is credited as scenario director.
Shinji Yamamoto is credited as scenario writer.

vfCrossroadsCharacters.title: Virtua Fighter Crossroads Characters and Roster
vfCrossroadsCharacters.intro: Virtua Fighter Crossroads introduces four new protagonists while bringing older Virtua Fighter characters into the same world. Current reveals include Cielo, Stella Bridges, Pai Chan, Wolf Hawkfield, Akira Yuki, and Bakunawa Killer.

fighters.name: Cielo
fighters.role: Cielo is the first protagonist players control in single-player and one of the four new leads whose path crosses through Vilasapara.

fighters.name: Stella Bridges
fighters.role: Stella Bridges is one of the four new protagonists and part of the new generation of Virtua Fighter characters.

fighters.name: Unnamed Protagonist 3
fighters.role: One of the four new protagonists whose life and goals intersect with the larger Vilasapara story.

fighters.name: Unnamed Protagonist 4
fighters.role: One of the four new protagonists with a separate background, motivation, and story tone.

fighters.name: Pai Chan
fighters.role: Pai Chan appears as an older legacy character and is connected to Cielo's pursuit of the Bakunawa Killer.

fighters.name: Wolf Hawkfield
fighters.role: Wolf returns as one of the legacy Virtua Fighter characters visible in Crossroads.

fighters.name: Akira Yuki
fighters.role: Akira appears as a returning Virtua Fighter figure in the Crossroads roster and world.

fighters.name: Bakunawa Killer
fighters.role: Bakunawa Killer is tied to a string of attacks targeting fighters throughout Vilasapara.

fighters.name: Jacky Bryant
fighters.role: Jacky is referenced through Vilasapara wall art alongside other legacy Virtua Fighter figures.

vfCrossroadsCombat.title: Virtua Fighter Crossroads Combat Mechanics
vfCrossroadsCombat.intro: Virtua Fighter Crossroads keeps the series focused on close-range reads while rebuilding the battle system around new momentum tools, cinematic defensive options, and a more realistic hit presentation. This section separates battle rules from individual mechanics so players can quickly see what each system does in actual fights.

mechanics.name: Standard Rules
mechanics.howItWorks: Victory is determined by depleting the opponent's health or causing a ring out. By default, matches are won by taking three rounds.
mechanics.playerImpact: This is the traditional Virtua Fighter-style rule option for players who want health management, stage control, and ring-out pressure.

mechanics.name: Uprising Rules
mechanics.howItWorks: A new rule option built around dynamic momentum-shifting opportunities, designed as a separate concept from Standard rules.
mechanics.playerImpact: Uprising is the key ruleset to watch for players who want a more dramatic match flow than classic round-based combat.

mechanics.name: Break and Rush
mechanics.howItWorks: Repeated hits to specific body regions build damage. Once enough damage accumulates, the attacker can land a break attack, and the broken status carries over across rounds.
mechanics.playerImpact: Players can plan attacks around body-part pressure instead of only chasing immediate damage, creating longer-term strategy across a match.

mechanics.name: Stunner
mechanics.howItWorks: A new strike technique that opens opponents to additional damage and connects into the new Stun Combo flow.
mechanics.playerImpact: Stunner gives players a clear way to create combo opportunities without relying only on older launch-and-juggle patterns.

mechanics.name: Stun Combo
mechanics.howItWorks: A new combo mechanic focused on fluidity and realism, built so players can access stronger combo routes without complex inputs.
mechanics.playerImpact: New players get a clearer path into damaging follow-ups, while experienced players can study how stun states reshape pressure.

mechanics.name: Flow Guard
mechanics.howItWorks: A stylish defensive mechanic emphasizing fluid motion and visual flair. It works alongside Break and Rush to shift offense and defense during combat.
mechanics.playerImpact: Defense becomes more active and readable, giving players another layer beyond simply blocking or evading.

mechanics.name: Close-Range Virtua Fighter Core
mechanics.howItWorks: Virtua Fighter combat is built around close-range fighting with striking, throwing, and guarding as the central pillars.
mechanics.playerImpact: Crossroads still asks players to read the opponent directly: strikes beat throw attempts, guard shuts down strikes, and throws punish passive guarding.

mechanics.name: Cinematic Hit Presentation
mechanics.howItWorks: Crossroads uses an action movie-inspired combat presentation with dramatic camera work and heavier close-up hit reactions.
mechanics.playerImpact: The game keeps Virtua Fighter's technical identity while making decisive attacks easier to read and more visually expressive.

mechanics.name: Rebuilt Intuitive Controls
mechanics.howItWorks: The development team is preserving Virtua Fighter's simple, immediate feel while reorganizing older complex elements and adding new systems.
mechanics.playerImpact: Crossroads is being positioned as simple to understand at first contact, with depth coming from timing, reads, defense, and system mastery.

vfCrossroadsBeginner.title: Virtua Fighter Crossroads Beginner Guide
vfCrossroadsBeginner.intro: Virtua Fighter is not about fireballs or long-range zoning. It is about close-range decisions: when to strike, when to guard, when to throw, and how to react after each exchange. This guide gives new players a clean training path before Crossroads launches.

steps.title: Learn the strike, throw, and guard triangle
steps.whatToLearn: Striking beats opponents who try to throw, guarding nullifies strikes, and throws open up opponents who keep guarding.
practiceActions: Play short rounds focused only on blocking, throwing, and basic attacks.
After every exchange, name what beat what before starting the next round.
steps.crossroadsCarryover: Crossroads keeps the close-range Virtua Fighter identity and adds new systems on top of that foundation.

steps.title: Build comfort with Punch, Kick, and Guard
steps.whatToLearn: Virtua Fighter notation uses simple button logic: PK means Punch then Kick, while P+K means pressing Punch and Kick at the same time.
practiceActions: Repeat basic Punch, Kick, Guard, PK, and P+K inputs until the timing feels natural.
Read move-list arrows from Player 1's facing direction, then reverse them when practicing on the opposite side.
steps.crossroadsCarryover: Crossroads is being designed around an intuitive control feel where basic actions remain easy to understand.

steps.title: Use Tutorial Mode before chasing advanced defense
steps.whatToLearn: Official beginner guidance recommends learning controls, movement, guarding, and fundamentals before advanced defensive and offensive lessons.
practiceActions: Run the basic tutorial first.
Ignore advanced systems during the first session and repeat movement plus guard drills.
steps.crossroadsCarryover: The same order works for Crossroads: learn the core controls first, then study Break and Rush, Flow Guard, Stunner, and Stun Combo.

steps.title: Separate Standing Guard from Crouching Guard
steps.whatToLearn: Standing Guard blocks high and mid attacks but loses to lows and throws. Crouching Guard blocks lows and avoids highs and throws, but loses to mids.
practiceActions: Start with Standing Guard to reduce damage from strong mid attacks.
Add Crouching Guard once you can recognize low attacks and throw attempts.
steps.crossroadsCarryover: Flow Guard adds a new defensive layer, but basic high, mid, low, and throw awareness still comes first.

steps.title: Attack after a successful guard
steps.whatToLearn: After a successful block, the blocking player often gets to move first. That is the moment to strike back instead of staying frozen.
practiceActions: Block a basic attack and immediately answer with a fast mid attack.
Practice one safe follow-up for your training character before adding more options.
steps.crossroadsCarryover: Flow Guard and cinematic combat presentation make defensive moments a major part of the Crossroads learning path, but the habit still starts with basic guard discipline.

steps.title: Do not hide behind guard forever
steps.whatToLearn: Guarding too much invites throws. Strike when you expect a throw, and practice throw escapes with the opponent's throw direction plus P+G or by pressing P while guarding.
practiceActions: Let an opponent throw you several times so you can see the pattern.
Alternate between guard, strike, and throw escape drills.
steps.crossroadsCarryover: The same strike-throw-guard reading game remains the fastest way to understand one-on-one Crossroads battles.

steps.title: Start with recommended combos, not long routes
steps.whatToLearn: After landing an attack, follow up with a simple combo first. Longer combo routes can raise damage, but they should come after the starter routes feel automatic.
practiceActions: Pick one launcher or starter and one short follow-up.
Repeat the same combo from both sides before adding a second route.
steps.crossroadsCarryover: Stunner and Stun Combo give Crossroads new combo paths, so clean starter habits will matter more than memorizing old routes without context.

steps.title: Go Tutorial Mode, Arcade Mode, then online matches
steps.whatToLearn: Official beginner guidance recommends Tutorial Mode for controls, Arcade Mode for roster familiarity, and online matches once confidence builds.
practiceActions: Use Tutorial Mode for inputs.
Use Arcade Mode to see many characters.
Use online matches only after you can guard, punish, and perform one short combo reliably.
steps.crossroadsCarryover: Battle Mode includes one-on-one fights online and offline, so this practice flow maps cleanly into direct versus play.

steps.title: Use beginner-friendly Virtua Fighter practice picks
steps.whatToLearn: Official current Virtua Fighter guidance highlights Jacky, Pai, and Jean as beginner-friendly choices for learning fundamentals in the existing game environment.
practiceActions: Use Jacky to learn simple strikes and throws.
Use Pai to learn stance and reversal awareness.
Use Jean to learn basic techniques and charge attacks.
steps.crossroadsCarryover: Use these as current Virtua Fighter practice picks; Crossroads character selection should be based on the final Crossroads roster resources.

steps.title: Carry single-player lessons into versus play
steps.whatToLearn: Crossroads is designed so what players learn in single-player can carry into competitive matches. The four protagonists are also planned as playable versus characters.
practiceActions: Use Story Mode to learn character identity and battle habits.
Take the same character into Battle Mode to test those habits in one-on-one fights.
steps.crossroadsCarryover: Cielo is a striking-focused MMA fighter rooted in boxing, making him a natural first character to watch for modern close-range pressure.

quickTips: Virtua Fighter Crossroads keeps the close-range punch, kick, and guard identity while adding Break and Rush, Flow Guard, Stunner, and Stun Combo.
Strike-throw-guard logic remains the fastest mental model for understanding one-on-one Crossroads battles.
Progress in order: Tutorial Mode for inputs, Arcade Mode for the roster, then online matches once you can guard, punish, and run one short combo.
Jacky, Pai, and Jean are beginner-friendly current Virtua Fighter picks for building fundamentals before the Crossroads roster is finalized.

vfCrossroadsBattleMode.title: Virtua Fighter Crossroads Battle Mode and Online Play
vfCrossroadsBattleMode.intro: Crossroads is built around two connected play styles: a narrative-heavy single-player adventure in Vilasapara and direct one-on-one battles through Battle Mode. Story battles teach the world, characters, and new systems, while Battle Mode gives players the classic head-to-head format.

modes.name: Story Mode / Fighting Adventure
modes.playerExperience: Explore Vilasapara, follow the intertwined stories of four protagonists, and make choices where actions, victories, and defeats shape the story.
confirmedFeatures: Side stories and quests tied to Vilasapara
modes.bestFor: Players who want story, exploration, character arcs, and battle encounters in one mode.

modes.name: Multi-Opponent Brawls
modes.playerExperience: Fight dramatic multi-opponent battles woven into the narrative instead of only traditional one-on-one duels.
modes.bestFor: Players coming from RGG-style action games who want broader combat encounters inside a Virtua Fighter framework.

modes.name: Boss Battles
modes.playerExperience: Face intense boss fights that punctuate major story moments and test the player's grasp of the new battle system.
modes.bestFor: Players who want climactic single-player fights with stronger presentation than standard match play.

modes.name: Battle Mode
modes.playerExperience: Jump directly into one-on-one matches without going through the story adventure flow.
modes.bestFor: Players who want direct Virtua Fighter matches, local play, online play, or focused system practice.

modes.name: Online Battle Mode
modes.playerExperience: Play head-to-head matches online through Battle Mode.
modes.bestFor: Players who want to test Crossroads characters and systems against other players.

modes.name: Offline Battle Mode
modes.playerExperience: Use Battle Mode for offline head-to-head fights, practice sets, and local match sessions.
modes.bestFor: Players who want local versus, lab sessions, or event setups without online matchmaking.

modes.name: Rulesets
modes.playerExperience: Standard keeps health depletion and ring-out victory conditions, while Uprising introduces a new momentum-shifting battle concept.
modes.bestFor: Players comparing classic Virtua Fighter match logic with the new Crossroads battle direction.

modes.name: Story-to-Versus Character Carryover
modes.playerExperience: Meet the four protagonists through the story, then take them into versus play.
confirmedFeatures: Four protagonists are planned as playable characters
Single-player learning is designed to carry into competitive matches
Cielo uses a striking-focused MMA style rooted in boxing
modes.bestFor: Players who want to choose a main through story familiarity before entering Battle Mode.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Virtua Fighter Crossroads

questions.question: When is Virtua Fighter Crossroads releasing?
questions.answer: Virtua Fighter Crossroads is scheduled for a 2027 launch window. SEGA has not announced an exact release date yet.

questions.question: What platforms will Virtua Fighter Crossroads be on?
questions.answer: Named platforms are still TBA. Virtua Fighter Crossroads is described as a home console-focused project, and SEGA will publish the full platform list closer to launch.

questions.question: Is Virtua Fighter Crossroads a fighting game or an adventure game?
questions.answer: Virtua Fighter Crossroads is a fighting adventure. It blends a narrative single-player story set in the city of Vilasapara with traditional one-on-one Virtua Fighter combat.

questions.question: Who are the protagonists in Virtua Fighter Crossroads?
questions.answer: The story follows four new protagonists, including Cielo and Stella Bridges, with legacy fighters like Pai Chan, Wolf Hawkfield, and Akira Yuki also appearing in Virtua Fighter Crossroads.

questions.question: Where does the Virtua Fighter Crossroads story take place?
questions.answer: The story is set in Vilasapara, a fictional Southeast Asian fighting city where the Vila Fight Fest, crime syndicates, and martial artists collide in Virtua Fighter Crossroads.

questions.question: Is Bakunawa Killer playable in Virtua Fighter Crossroads?
questions.answer: Yes. Bakunawa Killer is confirmed as a playable character in Battle Mode and appears in Cielo's story thread in Virtua Fighter Crossroads.
